content.problemStatement:
Let d(C)d(C)d(C) denote the number of **distinct** elements in array CCC.

For a given array BBB of length MMM, score(B)=max‚Ā°(d(C))\text{score}(B) = \max(d(C))score(B)=max(d(C)) across all CCC satisfying:


CCC consists of MMM positive integers;
1‚Č§Ci‚Č§Bi1\le C_i \le B_i1‚Č§Ci‚Äč‚Č§Bi‚Äč for all 1‚Č§i‚Č§M1\le i \le M1‚Č§i‚Č§M;
CCC is a **non-decreasing** array.
You are given an array AAA of size NNN and QQQ queries. For the ithi^{th}ith query:


Given two integers PiP_iPi‚Äč and XiX_iXi‚Äč, update APiA_{P_i}APi‚Äč‚Äč to XiX_iXi‚Äč;
Find ‚ąĎi=1N‚ąĎj=iNscore(A[i,j])\displaystyle{\sum_{i=1}^{N} \sum_{j=i}^{N} \text{score}(A[i,j])}i=1‚ąĎN‚Äčj=i‚ąĎN‚Äčscore(A[i,j]).
Note that the updates to array AAA are **persistent**. This means that each update to AAA carries over to subsequent queries.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains two positive integers NNN and QQQ ‚ąí-‚ąí the size of array AAA and the number of queries.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers, A1,A2‚Ä¶ANA_1, A_2 \ldots A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč‚Ä¶AN‚Äč ‚ąí-‚ąí denoting the array AAA.
Each of the next QQQ lines contains two space-separated integers PiP_iPi‚Äč and XiX_iXi‚Äč ‚Äď‚Äď‚Äď the description of the ithi^{th}ith query.


Output Format
For each query, output on a new line, the required sum after updating APiA_{P_i}APi‚Äč‚Äč.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
The sum of QQQ over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Given N=2N = 2N=2, Q=2Q = 2Q=2, and initial array A=[2,1]A = [2, 1]A=[2,1]. The queries are:

P1=2P_1 = 2P1‚Äč=2, X1=2X_1 = 2X1‚Äč=2: Update A2A_2A2‚Äč to 222, resulting in A=[2,2]A = [2, 2]A=[2,2]. We need to calculate sum of scores of all subarrays of AAA.

score([2])=1\text{score}([2]) = 1score([2])=1. There are two occurrences if subarray [2][2][2].
score([2,2])=2\text{score}([2,2]) = 2score([2,2])=2 since from array [2,2][2,2][2,2], we can create the array [1,2][1,2][1,2] with 222 distinct elements, satisfying all conditions.
Thus, the answer to the query is 1+1+2=41+1+2=41+1+2=4.


P2=1P_2 = 1P2‚Äč=1, X2=1X_2 = 1X2‚Äč=1: Update A1A_1A1‚Äč to 1, resulting in A=[1,2]A = [1, 2]A=[1,2]. We need to calculate sum of scores of all subarrays of AAA.

score([1])=score([2])=1\text{score}([1]) = \text{score}([2]) = 1score([1])=score([2])=1
score([1,2])=2\text{score}([1,2]) = 2score([1,2])=2 since from array [1,2][1,2][1,2], we can create the array [1,2][1,2][1,2] with 222 distinct elements, satisfying all conditions.
Thus, the answer to the query is 1+1+2=41+1+2=41+1+2=4.
